processContract.stdout: Successful JSON commands write exactly one JSON value to stdout unless an admitted explicit output-file flag writes that JSON value to the selected path and leaves stdout empty. Successful text or help commands write text to stdout unless an admitted explicit output-file flag writes the selected representation to the selected path and leaves stdout empty. Commands do not write stderr on success.
processContract.stderr: Argument parsing, unsupported command, input admission, and output serialization failures write human diagnostics to stderr. Failed report commands may still write a machine-readable failed report to stdout when admission succeeds. Opt-in agent-envelope repair packets may write deterministic invalid-input JSON to stdout with exit code 1 and empty stderr.

ref.format: safe repo-relative caller-owned file, report ref, or scanner root ref; scanner root refs may be .

browserMode.nonClaim: serve_local_view only adds --serve to requirement-browser-server routes; it does not create a public server or approve rendered artifacts.

knownChangedPaths.nonClaim: knownChangedPaths is diagnostic-only; selective routing requires a caller-owned changed_path_set input ref.

observedReports.blockingSemantics: any observed report state other than passed blocks command emission

openBrowser.nonClaim: openBrowser only adds --open when browserMode is serve_local_view.

nonClaims: This input contract describes agent-route admission only.
It does not validate referenced file contents, execute commands, read repositories, or approve merge.

outputContract.authority: deterministic route report derived from admitted agent-route input

outputContract.nonClaim: Init does not read repository files, discover tests, write files, create requirements, or approve merge/release/rollout.

nonClaims: The generated source is not a package-root SDK contract or npm subpath API.
The generated source does not resolve package pins, lockfiles, repository roots, installed binary paths, native witnesses, proof freshness, merge policy, or rollout policy.

inputContract.nonClaim: Migration parity admission does not approve old-owner deletion, execute native witnesses, authenticate evidence, or prove semantic equivalence.
Migration plans route caller-owned review work only and do not edit files, delete old owners, approve merge, or prove proof freshness.

inputContract.authority: caller-owned runtime dependency resolution and lockfile identity facts
inputContract.lockfileIntegrity: npm-style sha256, sha384, or sha512 integrity string admitted from caller-owned lockfile facts
inputContract.nonClaim: The command does not read lockfiles, resolve packages, authenticate registry access, execute witnesses, or prove proof freshness.

compatibility.schemaVersion: optional transitional top-level field; if present it must be 1

environmentPolicy.nonClaim: Repo-profile admission checks explicit caller-owned command policy facts and does not execute commands or discover repository files.

inputContract.authority: requirement coverage input compose input contract

normalized.nonClaim: Normalized mode preserves source-set provenance through normalizedTestEvidenceInventory.

direct.nonClaim: Direct mode admits passed child reports and rejects source-set or candidate inventory inputs that need an explicit normalization/materialization envelope.

nonClaims: Coverage input composition does not execute native tests, authenticate receipts, decide freshness, or approve merge.

outputContract.authority: requirement coverage input compose output contract
outputContract.provenanceRule: When normalizedTestEvidenceInventory is present, it preserves the admitted source-set envelope and its nested inventory must equal testEvidenceInventory.
nonClaims: Coverage input composition emits a direct requirement-coverage-view input and does not execute native tests, authenticate receipts, decide freshness, or approve merge.
Classification records describe Proofkit coverage diagnostics only; consumer repositories own severity policy, merge admission, escalation, and native witness execution.

outputContract.authority: requirement impact input compose output contract
nonClaims: Impact input composition emits a direct impact command input and does not execute native tests, authenticate receipts, decide freshness, or approve merge.

selectedSourceIds.format: non-empty array of stable source_id values when provided
selectedSourceIds.semantics: Restricts output to caller-selected source rows while preserving source-set order.

nonClaims: The command does not discover repository files, infer source completeness, execute witnesses, decide proof freshness, approve merge, or approve rollout.
Resolver projection is a shape-compatible data product, not proof freshness, native test execution, or consumer merge authority.

inputContract.callerTextAuthority: callerAnnotations fields are untrusted presentation annotations and never command-owned nonClaims
callerAnnotations fields are untrusted presentation annotations and never command-owned nonClaims

outputContract.authority: requirement spec tree view output contract
nonClaims: Requirement spec tree views are presentation projections only.
Requirement spec tree views do not scan repositories, compute source freshness, execute witnesses, approve merge, or replace the requirement spec tree admission report.

rules: paths must be sorted unique repository-relative POSIX paths
missing files must not include contentBase64
suppressions match exact findingClass, path, and line
suppression reason is report-visible caller text and is admitted through secret-like text rejection

inputContract.nonClaim: Secret scan does not discover repository files or replace provider secret scanning.

outputContract.authority: secret-like text pattern findings over admitted explicit inventory
outputContract.redactionRule: matched secret-like values are never emitted to stdout reports
outputContract.nonClaim: Findings are pattern detections, not credential validity proof.

relationRules: proofkit_text_policy requires commandId text-policy and reason text_policy
proofkit_secret_scan requires commandId secret-scan and reason secret_scan
caller_owned_external requires reason external_secret_scan and must not use Proofkit-owned command ids text-policy or secret-scan
scanObligation.nonClaim: Selective gate planning admits scan obligations but does not execute scanner commands or prove repository-wide credential absence.

inputContract.authority: test evidence inventory admission and projection admission

discovery-draft.authority: caller-owned test discovery draft projection admission
discovery-draft.nonClaim: Discovery draft reports candidate-only guidance from explicit caller-owned facts; it does not scan repositories, execute tests, emit semantic_falsifier evidence, or close coverage.

prefix.format: stable rule id namespace used to derive commandRefs

proof-binding-derived.nonClaim: This projection input does not make Proofkit the owner of requirement meaning, native test execution, semantic coverage, repository scanning, proof freshness, or merge policy.

strict-inventory.nonClaim: Strict inventory admission validates caller-owned evidence records; Proofkit does not execute those tests.

nonClaims: Projection modes are closed vocabulary: proof-binding-derived or discovery-draft.
Discovery draft candidate output must be materialized into strict inventory before it can affect coverage.
Proof-binding-derived routes remain proof_route_candidate entries until a consumer owner materializes an executable semantic oracle.
The derived inventory remains caller-owned input for downstream coverage views.

agentActionPlan.nonClaim: Agent actions are deterministic guidance over admitted caller-owned inventory facts and do not execute tests, approve merge, or decide semantic truth.

oracleFields.assertionSummary: non-empty assertion summary for semantic_falsifier, contract_admission, and property_or_fuzz entries
oracleFields.expectedPublicOutcome: non-empty expected public outcome for semantic_falsifier, contract_admission, and property_or_fuzz entries

nonClaims: Test evidence inventory classifications describe admitted caller-owned inventory facts and Proofkit-derived structural diagnostics only.
Proofkit does not infer weak tests from names, prose, mocks, sleeps, snapshots, or repository scans.

inputContract.authority: caller-owned manifest plus explicitly selected filesystem snapshot

item.pathAuthority: packageManifestPath names an explicit repo-relative package.json; every export condition names its explicit repo-relative non-JSX TypeScript sourcePath, and the canonical resolved source target must also have a .ts, .mts, or .cts extension
item.setFields: deniedExportKeys, runtimeExports, and typeExports are sorted unique arrays
item.exportConditionsRule: non-empty and sorted unique by condition

sourceGrammar.identifierDomain: direct ASCII identifiers in code; Unicode remains permitted inside comments and literals
admittedExportForms: named runtime re-exports from a string module specifier
named type re-exports from a string module specifier
ASCII-named function, class, enum, interface, type, const, let, and var exports
rejectedLexicalForms: slash tokens outside comments, including division and regular-expression literals
escaped or direct non-ASCII code identifiers
top-level angle-bracket syntax in variable export declarations

nonClaims: The command does not infer conventional package or source layouts.
The command does not parse JSX or admit TSX source files.
The command does not parse unrestricted TypeScript; sources outside the documented export grammar fail closed.
Caller-owned source-to-condition mappings do not prove compiler output provenance, checkout freshness, package-manager truth, merge readiness, or rollout.

inputContract.authority: witness plan input admission and projection admission

explicit-command-catalog.nonClaim: Explicit command catalogs are caller-owned; Proofkit admits command safety shape but does not execute commands.

admissionRules: vocabulary must pass witness command vocabulary admission
binding-derived projection requires exactly one admitted parallelGroup; multi-group vocabularies require an explicit witness command catalog
display command text must be display-only command text without shell control tokens, quoting, escaping, or secret-like tokens
each referenced environment class must admit networkPolicy none, credentialClass none, and cachePolicy disabled
requirement-bindings.nonClaim: Binding-derived witness-plan projection avoids duplicate command identity but does not prove command pass evidence or native witness freshness.

nonClaims: Workspace manifest facts project explicit caller-owned manifest records and do not scan repositories, read package files, interpret package-manager policy, execute commands, or approve merge.
